yesterdayAnalysis: Rest day as planned — exactly what you needed 2 days post-Oregon. Your body is still processing the 70.3 effort, and zero activity was the right call. Sleep improved to 84/100 with 9 hours and good REM recovery (139min), which shows the body is healing.
todayRecommendation: Easy 21.2mi ROUVY ride completed at HR 117 — appropriate recovery intensity. TSS 31 is light aerobic stimulus without digging a hole. This is exactly the kind of gentle movement that aids recovery without impeding it.
weekProgress: Week 1 of 13: 31 TSS of 150 target (21%) with 3 days remaining. This is MANDATORY post-race recovery week — low TSS is the plan, not a problem. You're following the recovery protocol correctly. The schedule shows big sessions still ahead (Thu run, Fri swim, Sat long ride), but do NOT feel obligated to hit 150 TSS this week if your body says otherwise.
keyInsight: You're recovering intelligently from Oregon — sleep is rebounding, HR is controlled, and you're resisting the urge to jump back into hard training too soon.
positives: Sleep quality climbing steadily: 49→76→84→88 over 4 nights post-race
Resting HR dropping from race peak (58) back toward baseline (50 last night)
